summary: Jeff Atwood announces his new position at Vertigo Software, a well-known Microsoft-focused development shop. He highlights the company's impressive portfolio including Quake II .NET, IBuySpy, and the .NET Pet Shop J2EE performance comparison. The post marks a significant career move requiring relocation from North Carolina to California. Atwood expresses genuine excitement about joining what he considers a high-profile team of developers.
key_points: Vertigo Software was known for high-profile Microsoft technology demonstrations and sample applications
The company's portfolio included gaming (Quake II .NET), e-commerce (IBuySpy, Pet Shop), and smart client applications
This job required a cross-country relocation from NC to CA
Atwood had previously referenced Vertigo's work in blog posts about managed code performance and J2EE comparisons
money_quotes: I'm tremendously excited to join such a high-profile group of software developers, and I can't wait to start.
key_insight: Sometimes career advancement means making big moves—both professionally and geographically—to work with teams whose work you admire.
